### Post by sparkplug on 2006-10-29

Option      "SWCursor" "on"
Option      "HWCursor" "off"
to your /etc/X11/xorg.conf,Section "Device".

### Post by blitzer on 2006-11-06
Hello Coop,

The only time I had problems installing 3D Accl. using method 1 [COLOR="Blue"]FOR DAPPER[/COLOR] was the moving of this file [COLOR="Blue"]"download this file: libGL.so.1.2 and then copy it to the /usr/lib/ directory." 
[/COLOR]

Did you download this file "libgl.so.1.2" and copied it to the directory that troubleshooting method 1 stated ?  I had to do it under root to be able to move that file ](*,) 

:idea: Upgrading to Edgy Eft and using the "Radeon" Free software drivers(see Sig below)has great 3D Acceleration.  Once you have driver setup just add [BerylOnEdgy]("https://help.ubuntu.com/community/BerylOnEdgy")  

[IMG][[IMG]http://img454.imageshack.us/img454/3944/berylonedgyuo8.th.png[/IMG]](http://img454.imageshack.us/my.php?image=berylonedgyuo8.png)[/IMG]  


This is very important:
As you follow the steps make sure you do "Troubleshooting for Method 1"

[COLOR="Red"]How to: Getting root privliges to copy and or move files (if an easier way please post it)

Enabling the root account
In console
Code:

sudo passwd root

Enter password for root
Confirm password for root
Restart and log in using username:root password: password you made
[/COLOR]
Disabling the root account
Code:

sudo passwd -l root

Good luck !

### Post by kvonb on 2006-11-07
1) The 9200 does not work with any of the xgl/compiz stuff
2) The 8.28 driver (in Edgy) has problems with the 9200, ie google earth and Open Office!
3) Here is my installation script, download, unzip it to your home folder and run it with:

```
sudo ./ati-install.sh
```

Regards,  Kev :)

oh and....

4) If you can afford it, dump the 9200 and buy nVidia!

### Post by Coop on 2006-11-07
Hello.I am using Dapper.I downloaded the script,unzipped it to my home folder and ran it.
When I ran the script it downloaded libGL.so.1.2 (although the downloading didn't look proper because it didn't tell the file size and how much time was left in downloading it),but after it downloaded libGL.so.1.2 it said:```
libGL.so.1.2 found, checking file size...
file size seems to be incorrect, try downloading again!

```
What should I do?

---

### Post by kvonb on 2006-11-07
..try running the script again, or download my copy from the link below and place it in the ATI-8-27-10-install folder which should be in your home folder or wherever you ran the script from and then run the script again.

---

### Post by Coop on 2006-11-07
Thank you.

---

### Post by kvonb on 2006-11-07
The same goes for the ati driver download, if it fails, just run the script again.

Let me know if it works for you, I've used this script for 5 months and it works well for me.

The ATI 9200 is getting a bit old now, the 8.28 driver (as supplied in Edgy) is the last version to support the 9xxx series of cards, and it has a few problems with certain progs, for example Google Earth and Open Office, that's why my script installs the 8.27 driver .

Regards,  Kev

---

### Post by warjowuch on 2006-11-08
Hi there,
I have similar problems, allthough I did have 3d working on my 9200SE, both with fglrx AND radeon (xserver-xorg-video-ati) drivers. But somehow after a while, they stopped working after I put some lines in my xorg.conf.
When I took those away, it still did not work.

I allready know that GLX does not load, but it should load according to my xorg.conf. I have direct rendering, glxgears work pretty good and so does fglrxinfo say.

lsmod | grep glx says nothing, lsmod | grep fglrx says it is loaded.

I have created a symlink to the troubleshooter from installguide with
>  sudo ln -s /usr/lib/dri /usr/lib/xorg/modules/dri


I also disabled composite at the end with 'Option  "Composite" "0"' and have the DRI-thinghy with mode 0666. WHatever that does.

how do I remove this by the way?

So, does anyone know what to do? Oh, fglrx-driver is also loaded and checked in xorg.conf (off course, how could it be listed elsewise with lsmod :-))

(edit: completed information with composite and dri-stuff)

### Post by Coop on 2006-11-09
If I get a Nvidia card will it also have these problems or will it work without any problems?Also are there any other good graphic card maufacturers whose cards work with that 3d AIGLX/Beryl/Compiz/XGL stuff?

---

### Post by igknighted on 2006-11-09
> **Coop said:**
> If I get a Nvidia card will it also have these problems or will it work without any problems?Also are there any other good graphic card maufacturers whose cards work with that 3d AIGLX/Beryl/Compiz/XGL stuff?

ATI's are generally regarded as the most troublesome cards in linux.  NVIDIA's have their own issues as well however.  The only computer that I have ever had just work as far as graphics was my girlfriend's dell that uses Intel integrated graphics.  Personally, I like my ATI card, and I have learned how to tweak it properly (x800gto), and whenever I have to mess w/ NVIDIA I find it very frustrating.  It is all personal preference really.  That said, I used to use an ATI 9200, which is probably the second most troublesome card you can get (after the x200... esp the mobile version).  I would pay close attention to make sure the specific card you are using has no major issues, but as far as ATI and NVIDIA (imho) both can be equally as good choices.

---

### Post by darrenm on 2006-11-09
> **Coop said:**
> Hello.I am running Dapper.Also please tell me how to make a link that says something else instead of the linked website's address.

Try ```
sudo aticonfig --initial --force
``` Then reboot. You seem to have 2 device sections.

Question #2: type
```
[url = http://www.a_website.com]This is a link to a website[/url]
``` but remove the spaces around the = sign

### Post by darrenm on 2006-11-11
Hello again.

Try 
```
sudo module-assistant update,prepare
sudo module-assistant build,install fglrx-kernel
```
Then reboot.

---

### Post by Coop on 2006-11-11
Hello.When I try the command you told me it says:```
sudo module-assistant update,prepare
Password:
sudo: module-assistant: command not found
```
and:```
sudo module-assistant build,install fglrx-kernel
sudo: module-assistant: command not found
```
Please help me.I will be highly obliged for your help.

---

### Post by darrenm on 2006-11-11
Ok sorry, you don't have module-assistant installed.

```
sudo apt-get install module-assistant
```

Then try my previous instructions.

### Post by kvonb on 2006-11-11
Hi Coop,

try this:

sudo gedit /etc/default/linux-restricted-modules-common

at the end of the file add this:

DISABLED_MODULES="fglrx"

Save, exit, then reboot.

Just a thought, do you have compiz installed?  Check in Synaptic using the search button, if you have, remove it.  You might also want to remove any of the aiglx/glx/compiz/beryl stuff that you installed and start again with the ATI driver.

### Post by kvonb on 2006-11-11
Try this:

sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf

add:

```
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
        Option      "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"

```
to the end of this section:

```
Section "Device"
        Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
        Driver      "fglrx"
EndSection
```

It should look like this:

```
Section "Device"
        Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
        Driver      "fglrx"
        Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
        Option      "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
EndSection
```

Save and exit.

Now open Synaptic (menu -> system -> administration -> synaptic package manager)

Click on the search icon and change it from "look in -> decription and name" to "look in -> name", type in:

dri

and click on search.

Right click on "libgl-mesa-dri" and select "mark for re-installation".  Do the same for "linux-dri-modules-common", then apply.

That should re-install all the DRI stuff.

Reboot, then open a terminal and run:

fglrxinfo

then:

ls -hAlF /lib/linux-restricted-modules/

then:

uname -r

and post the output?

thanks....Kev

### Post by kvonb on 2006-11-12
OK, try this in a term:

sudo mv /etc/apt/sources.list /etc/apt/sources.list.backup

Copy the following:

deb [http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu](http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu) dapper main restricted universe multiverse
deb [http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu](http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu) dapper-backports main restricted universe multiverse
deb [http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu](http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu) dapper-updates main restricted universe multiverse
deb [http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu](http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu) dapper-security main restricted universe multiverse

..then type:

sudo gedit /etc/apt/sources.list


Paste the above into gedit, save and exit

now type:

sudo apt-get update

then:

sudo apt-get dist-upgrade

then:

sudo apt-get install linux-dri-modules-'uname-r'

then:

sudo apt-get install --reinstall libgl1-mesa-dri

Let me know what happens.

### Post by CarpKing on 2006-11-13
> **Coop said:**
> I can't find the package linux-dri-modules-'uname-r' in Synaptic.

I believe 'uname-r' refers to the version of the kernal you're running.  So search in Synaptic for "linux-dri-modules" and install whichever one matches your kernal (it will probably be the latest).

### Post by kvonb on 2006-11-15
CooP,

I've been through everything I can think of, I'm at the end of my knowledge on this one, sorry.

What I suggest now is that you try booting the Edgy CD and see if your card is working on that, it should work on standard boot, mine did.

See this guide:

[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/RadeonDriver](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/RadeonDriver)

If it is, then you might want to consider installing Edgy, if the output of:  ```
glxinfo | grep "direct rendering"
``` doesn't show ```
direct rendering: Yes
```, then I suggest there is a hardware problem somewhere.

You will have to download the CD image and it is quite big unfortunately.


I wish I could help you further and I certainly hope that you get some good results.

Regards,  Kev

### Post by Circus-Killer on 2006-11-16
i havent had a chance to read the whole thread, so excuse me if this has been suggested already. i have the same card and have chosen to stick with the open source drivers that get installed with edgy.

the reason is that the open source drivers have full 3d support and composite support. ive tested *enemy territory* using the open source drivers and it works fine. so does beryl. so all in all, i see no need for using the official ati drivers.

with 9200 you have a choice, go with old official with no composite support, or use the open source drivers. the absolute latest official drivers do not support 9200 anymore.

so, as i said, i recommend open source drivers. worked for me. and is already installed when you do a fresh install of edgy.

laters.

### Post by Architeuthis on 2006-11-23
Hello I too have an ati radeon 9200 Pro, the official ati drivers (flgrx) were complete crap to me (errors with specific programs) so I tried out the guide for the open source drivers mentioned before. They gave me strange errors. I hoped they would be solved after upgrading to edgy, which wasn't the case. A new error related to Mesa came up, many people have this error too (this is the error i'm speaking about: "libGL warning: 3D driver claims to not support visual 0x5b"). Since this bug is not yet solved I think you will have this problem too. 
After all this misery I just put my old geforce 4 Mx 440 back in my PC.
But the guide (about the open source drivers) has completely been rewritten lately so I advise you to stick with dapper and try out the open source drivers, or reinstall dapper because someone suggested there may be something wrong with your install.

### Post by kvonb on 2006-12-01
Which version of the driver did you install? All support for 9200 based cards and younger is dropped in anything above 8.28.8!

Use his first method using the 8.28 driver, or better still follow my guide and use the opensource driver here:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=301364](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=301364)

It is for the liveCD but will work the same on your standard Edgy install (I assume you are running Edgy?) and you DON'T have to do the Beryl part if you don't want it.

Regards, KEv

### Post by kvonb on 2006-12-01
Change the "Driver" line back to "ati" or "radeon", or simpler still if you have an original copy of your xorg.conf, try using that.  There should be the original one in /etc/X11/ renamed something else, just poke around in there and see.

Once you change it back to standard, you might have to restart to load the ati module, try the test for direct rendering:

```
glxinfo | grep direct
```

If it says "Yes", then you have the opensource ati driver working.

It's crazy that our cards should be put on the scrapheap by ATI, seems like they're turning into a profit only company and forgetting their "older" products.  You'd think as an upstart company in the competitive video card market they'd try that bit harder!

Hope you have luck, KEv :)

### Post by kvonb on 2006-12-02
I think you might need to remove the GLX stuff, the ATI cards use AIGLX which is the default in Edgy.  Unfortunately removing the GLX stuff is beyond my knowledge, maybe just do a reversal of the installation instructions you used.

All I can suggest is to try my guide with the LiveCD and see how that goes, at least you will know if it works!

I know that's not much help, but it might give you a lead in the right direction.  If nothing else, it will give you a good clean xorg.conf.

Regards, KEv :)

### Post by ironstorm on 2006-12-09
I just wasted many hours trying to figure out what the hell is wrong with this card, X DRI and the Radeon OSS driver...  

The key is here: [https://bugs.freedesktop.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1700](https://bugs.freedesktop.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1700) (Bug in X.org v6.8.0+)

in your **Device Section** of your xorg.conf:
[INDENT]Section "Device"
...existing stuff...
  **Option "BusType" "PCI" ** # Radeon 9200 will complain of no AGP and disable DRI without this! [/INDENT]

then restart X with something like:
  [INDENT]sudo /etc/init.d/kdm restart # or gdm if you use that[/INDENT]

I'll be posting a blog entry on this and how to get this card to do 1600x1200 on my blog [http://stacktrace.org/](http://stacktrace.org/) tomorrow.

---
